en: Cousin Cora. I think you can call me Cora now.
ko: 설형문자의 발전이

en: I wouldn't dare with Cousin Robert. You must dare with me.
ko: 이 서판에 기록된 문자 언어가 인류 역사상 가장 위대한, 아마도 가장 위대한 발명이라고 하셨죠

en: How can I help? I wanted to tell you that I've settled the
ko: 이 서판에 기록된 문자 언어가 인류 역사상 가장 위대한, 아마도 가장 위대한 발명이라고 하셨죠

en: surprise for his birthday. Since you won't tell me what it is, I don't know what to say. Good.
ko: 소리를 서판에 인코딩하는 그 구체적인 요소가

en: Well, the thing is, I need Carson to keep the secret. Do you think he will? It's only
ko: 천재적인 발명이라는 겁니다. 그림을 그리는 건 이해가 되죠. 여기 보리가 있고

en: till the day, and it's only till after dinner. Mrs. Hughes is the one for a secret.
ko: 여기 태양, 여기 뭐든. 실제 물체를 그리는 건요. 하지만 소리를 기록하는 건

en: Yes, that's true. And it'll be she who has to deal with it.
ko: 천재적인 발명이죠. 상당히 역설적이라고 생각하는데 왜냐하면 처음

en: A band? Shh. No one must know. We'll get the carpets
ko: 한 세대 정도의 서판은 그림문자로 쓰여 있어서 각 기호가 보이는 대로의 의미를 갖거든요

en: rolled up and everything arranged during dinner, so that when his lordship walks out of the
ko: 메시지를 기록하는 매우 제한된 방법이었고 문법을 기록하는 데는

en: dining room, there it is. And we're to hide them until then?
ko: 메시지를 기록하는 매우 제한된 방법이었고 문법을 기록하는 데는

en: Well, hide them? You just have to keep them with you. His lordship won't come down here,
ko: 적합하지 않았죠. 그리고 고고학적으로 이해하는 두 번째 단계는

en: will he? So I have to find them food and beds. How
ko: 이 기호들이 여전히 보이는 대로의 의미를 갖지만 동시에 단어의 소리도

en: many are there? Six, I think. But I'll check.
ko: 이 기호들이 여전히 보이는 대로의 의미를 갖지만 동시에 단어의 소리도

en: And her ladyship knows about it? Well, not that it's a band, because it's
ko: 나타낼 수 있다는 인식이었습니다. 그래서 언어의 모든 소리를 표현하는 멋진 음절 기호들이 생기고

en: a surprise for her, too. But she knows that there is a surprise, and that you're helping
ko: 나타낼 수 있다는 인식이었습니다. 그래서 언어의 모든 소리를 표현하는 멋진 음절 기호들이 생기고

en: me with it. Very well. I'll see what I can do.
ko: 단어와 문법 등 모든 걸 기록할 수 있게 된 거죠. 중요한 건

en: Thank you. I wonder why Lady Rose was down here.
ko: 앗시리아학에서 받아들여진 법칙은 그림이 먼저고

en: And I'm afraid you will continue to wonder. That's very mysterious, Mrs. Hughes.
ko: 소리가 나중이라는 거였어요

en: You know me, Mr. Barrow. A woman of mystery, if ever there was one.
ko: 글쎄, 저는 이걸 믿기 매우 어렵습니다. 왜냐하면 만약

en: But her secret won't affect us. How can you be so sure?
ko: 글쎄, 저는 이걸 믿기 매우 어렵습니다. 왜냐하면 만약

en: Now, you have got me worried. I am sorry to hear that, Mr. Barrow. But now
ko: 모든 사람이 이해하고 사용할 수 있는 표면 위의 표시 체계를 만드는 것이

en: you must let me get on. How's my birthday dinner coming on?
ko: 소리를 나타내는 기호로 시작하지 않겠습니까? 모두가 같은

en: How do you know about the dinner? The cult hosts rang yesterday and said they'd
ko: 언어를 쓰니까요. ABC는 없었지만 쉽게 모든 모음과 자음을

en: love to come. What if we all swore to secrecy?
ko: 언어를 쓰니까요. ABC는 없었지만 쉽게 모든 모음과 자음을

en: God, people are so hopeless. I don't mind. It won't spoil it if it's not
ko: 언어를 쓰니까요. ABC는 없었지만 쉽게 모든 모음과 자음을

en: a surprise. Not for me. It may still be a surprise.
ko: 이름 붙이지 않고도 구성 요소를 파악할 수 있었을 겁니다. 그래서

en: So I should hope. Lady Rose, can I help?
ko: 소리를 만드는 기호를 가질 수 있었죠. 우리는 26개인데 그들이 50개를 정했다면

en: Oh, please don't let me disturb you. But I wanted to make a speech. Mrs. Hughes may
ko: 추가적인 어려움 없이 뭐든 쓸 수 있었을 겁니다. 그래서

en: have told you. I haven't yet.
ko: 가장 유연하지 않고 적응력이 떨어지는 그림문자 체계로 시작한 게

en: Well, we should tell them now. As a treat for his lordship, a London band is coming
ko: 가장 유연하지 않고 적응력이 떨어지는 그림문자 체계로 시작한 게

en: to play after dinner. A London band? That's the Berries.
ko: 매우 당혹스럽습니다

en: From a nightclub called The Lotus. A nightclub, milady? Really?
ko: 매우 당혹스럽습니다

en: But it must be a complete surprise. No one knows anything and they mustn't. That is,
ko: 그리고 소리로 넘어갔다는 거죠. 왜 그걸 했는지 모르겠어요. 제 직감은

en: her ladyship knows that something's going to happen, but even she doesn't know what.
ko: 우리가 가진 고고학적 증거가 궁극적으로 오해를 불러일으킨다는 겁니다

en: And you think she'll be pleased? She'll be thrilled.
ko: 우리가 가진 고고학적 증거가 궁극적으로 오해를 불러일으킨다는 겁니다

en: We'll look after your secret. So until then, if you can just make them comfortable.
ko: 아마 수메르인 이전에 매우 오랜 시간 동안

en: I know musicians are outside your daily round. Don't worry about that, milady. We can take
ko: 아마 수메르인 이전에 매우 오랜 시간 동안

en: it in our stride. We may be Yorkshiremen, but we do know a little of life in the city.
ko: 중동 세계의 사람들이 서로 접촉하고

en: Hello? Is anyone there? I think this is where we're supposed to be.
ko: 사람들 사이의 오랜 소통 체계가 있었을 거라 생각해요

en: Welcome to Downton. I'm catching the lady's eye.
ko: 그림이면 충분했으니까요. 상인이 양 세 마리를 팔려고 오면

en: Oh, no. Rose? No, we're not splitting tonight. We're all
ko: 양 그림 세 개를 그리면 얼마인지 뭔지 알 수 있었죠

en: going out together. What on earth is she talking about?
ko: 그래서 수메르인의 그림문자 기호는

en: Quick, pay. Anna, one, two, three.
ko: 매우 매우 오랜 기간의 끝자락에 있는 것이고

en: Happy birthday, Cousin Robert.
ko: 누군가가 이 제한된 표지판 같은 기호들을 가지고 언어를 쓸 수 있다고

en: I'm just wild about Harry, and Harry's wild about me.
ko: 생각한 거죠. 그게 제 생각이에요. 논란이 되는 주장인가요? 매우 논란이 됩니다

en: The heavenly blisses of his kisses fill me with ecstasy.
ko: 자연스러우니까요. 생각해보면 자연스러운 거예요

en: He's sweet just like chocolate candy, and just like honey from bees.
ko: 수메르어에서

en: Who is this singer, and how did he get here? Isn't it rather odd?
ko: 재미있는 문자 체계가 있는데요. '가다'라는 뜻의 어근 du가 있고 접두사로

en: No, I think it's fun.
ko: e, mu, ba 같은 것이 붙어요. 하나는 수동, 하나는 능동이고. 문장을 만들면

en: But Granny, is it really suitable that Rose has brought this man here?
ko: mu, ba, e 접두사 중 하나를 쓰고 어근을 쓰고 끝에 뭔가를 붙여요

en: My dear, we country dwellers must beware of being provincial. Try and let your time in
ko: 교착어라고 부르죠. 중심 단어에 앞뒤로 요소를 붙이는 거예요. 각 입자가 의미를 만들어서

en: London rub off on you a little more.
ko: '할 수 있었다면 했을 텐데 못 했다' 같은 긴 동사가 나오죠. 핵심은 이걸 쓰려면

en: It is a bit wild, jazz at Downton Abbey.
ko: 첫 번째로 mu 기호를 만들고 ba, e를 만들겠죠

en: I think it's lovely. You see, Tom, things can happen at Downton that no one imagined
ko: 사람들이 5분마다 그런 소리를 내니까요. 무슨 말인지 알겠죠? 네, 완전히요

en: even a few years ago. Take heart from that before you throw in the towel.
Is this your first experience of jazz, Lady Grantham?
Oh, is that what it is? Do you think any of them know what the others are playing?
Did you ask me to come down? I did. We've made some sandwiches for you and the others
I doubt you'll be off-duty before midnight. We'll set a little table inside the Green Bay store.
They sound good from down here. If you like that sort of thing.
I thought Mr Ross was very nice.
So did I. Strange to relate. Though it's still an odd sort of thing to be happening at Downton.
It makes you want to jig about, though, doesn't it? Certainly not.
I think we've done enough now, you know, to show that we're good sports.
Of course, of course. I do think it was a brilliant idea.
But it must be costing Rose a fortune. Shall we chip in?
Or pay it. Get the bandleader to send me the bill.
I'll go down at the end of the night and catch him before it's too late. Before he leaves.
I'm afraid I don't share your enthusiasm for her. Why not?
She's the type who demands all this is of right. But she wants it on a plate.
She won't work for it and she won't fight for it.
And that type doesn't deserve to survive. I don't want to make trouble.
She feels much the same about you.
Oh, Edith. I wish you'd tell me what's wrong.
How do you know something's wrong? Because I'm your mother.
Is it Michael? Is there any news? Not really.
He went to Munich a few weeks ago. That's all they know.
Well, of course you're worried.
Well, it's not often that a birthday surprise really is a surprise.
I just hope we haven't shocked the servants too much. Carson was ready to faint.
Although, amazingly, he told me that the band leader was a very decent fellow, my lord.
So perhaps we'll make a modern of him after all. I doubt it.
Is anyone still awake? Mary?
What are you doing down here? I was looking for Mr. Ross.
Mr. Ross, I wanted to thank you for a marvellous evening and also to ask you if you would be kind enough
to send the bill to his lordship. But that's not necessary. He wants to.
He says your present was arranging the whole thing.
Of course. And now it's my turn to thank you.
I've been so well looked after here. I'm glad to hear it.
Well, good night.
You
